['SNAIC capabilities - Assigns the right Technical Lead for the first consultation

**Output of this step:** Initial understanding of needs and assigned Technical Lead.



### Step 2: 1-to-1 Consultation

**Step name:** 1-to-1 Consultation

**Purpose:** Explore whether AI can address the company’s problem statements.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC and the company have an in-depth discussion - They identify where AI solutions can create significant value - They clarify possible use cases and expectations

**Output of this step:** Initial alignment on business problem, AI opportunities, and expected value.



### Step 3: Project Scoping

**Step name:** Project Scoping

**Purpose:** Define the roadmap for integrating AI into operations.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC AI experts work with the company - Project milestones are defined - Deliverables are defined - The AI adoption roadmap is outlined

**Output of this step:** Project roadmap, milestones, and deliverables.



### Step 4: Resource Identification

**Step name:** Resource Identification

**Purpose:** Determine the resources, expertise, budget, and funding mechanism needed.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC and the company identify required talent, technology, and budget - Both parties align on a suitable funding mechanism - Both parties confirm resource contributions

**Possible funding mechanisms:** - Company-funded project - Joint grant application by company and SIT - Industry Doctorate or Industry Master’s project - IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme - Integrative Team Project (ITP)

**SNAIC resource contribution may include:** - AI talent from SIT and NVIDIA - Postgraduate students - Undergraduate students - Key AI technologies - NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources

**Industry partner contribution may include:** - Domain experts - Problem statements - Available data - Funding

**Output of this step:** Agreed resources, funding route, and role allocation.



### Step 5: Project Development

**Step name:** Project Development

**Purpose:** Build and refine an AI solution.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC works closely with the company team - A minimum viable project (MVP) is developed - AI models are selected - AI models are customised and refined - The solution is tailored to the company’s needs and objectives

**Output of this step:** Working MVP and refined AI solution design.



### Step 6: Project Deployment or Scaling

**Step name:** Project Deployment or Scaling

**Purpose:** Move the MVP into production or expand it.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC advances the MVP into a production-ready solution - The solution is deployed or scaled - SNAIC provides continuous support

**Output of this step:** Production-ready solution or scaled deployment with ongoing support.

## Canonical Description

SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between SIT and NVIDIA. It helps companies adopt AI through applied research, AI solution development, and talent development. It also gives SIT students and professionals hands-on experience with real-world AI projects.



## One-Sentence Summary

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.



## Mission

### Mission 1: Research

Advance AI adoption by companies through applied research.

### Mission 2: Industry

Support industry capability development and AI applications development.

### Mission 3: Talent

Train skilled personnel for local industries, including students and working professionals.



## Vision

Bring the potential of AI into business operations so organisations can improve efficiency, intelligence, creativity, and innovation.

## Core Problems SNAIC Helps Address

### Problem 1: Technology barriers

Companies may face challenges related to data availability, cost, tooling, and technical complexity.



### Problem 2: Computational resource barriers

Companies may lack access to suitable hardware, GPUs, or cloud compute.



### Problem 3: Talent barriers

Companies may not have enough skilled, industry-ready AI talent.



## SNAIC Solution Areas

### Solution 1: Enhance domain knowledge

SNAIC combines AI expertise with industry domain knowledge.



### Solution 2: Integrate real-time data with Generative AI

SNAIC helps organisations connect operational data with modern AI systems.



### Solution 3: Cost-efficient AI solutions

SNAIC develops practical AI solutions that are aligned to business value and resource constraints.



## Industry Sectors Supported

·    Healthcare

·    Education

·    Logistics

·    Finance

·    Manufacturing

·    Telecommunication

·    Transportation

·    Public sector and infrastructure



## Strategic Pillars

### Pillar 1: AI Application Development for Industry

SNAIC works with companies to co-develop practical AI solutions for real use cases.

### Pillar 2: AI Talent Development

SNAIC develops AI talent through Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, undergraduate training, and upskilling for industry employees.

### Pillar 3: Translational AI Research

SNAIC conducts applied research that bridges academic innovation and industry implementation.



## Collaboration Model

### Model Summary

SNAIC follows a “1 (SIT) + 1 (NVIDIA) to many companies” model.

### Model Benefits

·    End-to-end AI translation of impactful AI solutions

·    Co-supervision by SIT and NVIDIA experts

·    Increased AI adoption across industries

·    AI competency development for students and working professionals

·    Stronger AI talent pipeline

·    Multi-Modal Models

·    Natural Language Processing (NLP)

**Goal:** Train more than 200 fresh graduates and mid-career professionals over 3 years to become AI practitioners.

**Structure:** 1. 2 months of intensive training in GenAI, LLMs, and Agentic AI. 2. 4 months of work on real-life projects with industry partners.

**Outcome:** Participants gain current AI skills, practical project experience, and exposure to industry use cases.

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

This shift embodies our commitment to sustainable and accessible commuting options for everyone at our vibrant new location!
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.



## Mission

### Mission 1: Research

Advance AI adoption by companies through applied research.

### Mission 2: Industry

Support industry capability development and AI applications development.

### Mission 3: Talent

Train skilled personnel for local industries, including students and working professionals.



## Vision

Bring the potential of AI into business operations so organisations can improve efficiency, intelligence, creativity, and innovation.

## Core Problems SNAIC Helps Address

### Problem 1: Technology barriers

Companies may face challenges related to data availability, cost, tooling, and technical complexity.



### Problem 2: Computational resource barriers

Companies may lack access to suitable hardware, GPUs, or cloud compute.



### Problem 3: Talent barriers

Companies may not have enough skilled, industry-ready AI talent.



## SNAIC Solution Areas

### Solution 1: Enhance domain knowledge

SNAIC combines AI expertise with industry domain knowledge.



### Solution 2: Integrate real-time data with Generative AI

SNAIC helps organisations connect operational data with modern AI systems.



### Solution 3: Cost-efficient AI solutions

SNAIC develops practical AI solutions that are aligned to business value and resource constraints.

### Pillar 2: AI Talent Development

SNAIC develops AI talent through Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, undergraduate training, and upskilling for industry employees.

### Pillar 3: Translational AI Research

SNAIC conducts applied research that bridges academic innovation and industry implementation.

Question: What are the admissions’ criteria?
Answer: SIT adopts an aptitude-based approach in assessing applicants for admission. Applicants are evaluated on their academic qualifications, co-curricular activity records and work experience. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an admission interview.

Applicants presenting the following qualifications can apply:
- A Diploma from any local Polytechnics in Singapore. 
- A Diploma from other institutions such as NAFA, LASALLE, BCA Academy and ITE will be considered on a case-by-case basis for selected programmes.
- The Singapore-Cambridge A-Levels with passes in at least 2 H2 subjects and offered General Paper or Knowledge & Inquiry (KI) in the same sitting (for selected courses).
- The International Baccalaureate with a grade five for at least 2 Higher Level (HL) and 1 Standard Level (SL) subject and had obtained the IB Diploma (for selected courses).
- International Qualifications with at least 12 years of formal education deemed as acceptable, equivalent qualifications for selected courses.

Certain programmes may also have programme-specific admission requirements. Applicants should refer to SingaporeTech.edu.sg for more information and ensure that all programme-specific requirements are met in order to be considered for admission.

- Reason for appealing in approximately 200 words.
- Supporting documents (in a single file, not exceeding 5 MB).

SIT uses an aptitude-based approach that evaluates academic qualifications, co-curricular activity records, and work experience. Shortlisted applicants are invited for an admission interview.

SIT accepts the following qualifications:
- A Diploma from any local Polytechnic in Singapore.
- A Diploma from other institutions such as NAFA, LASALLE, BCA Academy, and ITE (considered case-by-case for selected programmes).

- International Qualifications with at least 12 years of formal education deemed acceptable and equivalent (for selected courses).

For detailed admission requirements, please visit: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/requirements.

Question: What is the minimum Cut-Off Point for SIT?
Answer: SIT practises an aptitude-based admissions selection process where applicants are not only assessed based on their academic results; we will also review their co-curricular interests, work/internship experiences, portfolios/ essays (if applicable) and interview performances, just to name a few. As such, there is no minimum cut-off point and we strongly encourage all who meet our admission requirements, and who are interested in respective programmes to apply.

Even so, selection for admissions is a competitive process for a limited number of places, and it is based on relative merit (both academically and non-academically). To keep prospective students informed of SIT's respective programmes and their chances of gaining acceptance into SIT, we have published the SIT [Indicative Grade Profile](https://singaporetech.edu.sg/files/igp) as a guide.

Question: What are the admissions’ criteria?
Answer: SIT adopts an aptitude-based approach in assessing applicants for admission. Applicants are evaluated on their academic qualifications, co-curricular activity records and work experience. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an admission interview.

Applicants presenting the following qualifications can apply:
- A Diploma from any local Polytechnics in Singapore.

- International Qualifications with at least 12 years of formal education deemed as acceptable, equivalent qualifications for selected courses.

user_input: What are the delivery methods for SIT recorded video assessments, the criteria for Zoom interviews, and the available Portal payment methods?
reference:
- Delivery method for recorded video assessments: recorded and submitted through SIT’s video assessment platform; shortlisted applicants will receive instructions via email (you may be assigned more than one assessment if shortlisted for multiple programmes).  
- Criteria for Zoom interviews: used as an alternative when on-site face‑to‑face interviews cannot be conducted; only shortlisted candidates are invited and specific instructions are provided in interview invites.  
- Portal payment methods: PayNow, Credit Card or Debit Card via the SIT Application Portal.

Question: Are there overseas opportunities?
Answer: Yes! SIT students do have the opportunity to go on an overseas exchange as part of their studies.

Students pursuing joint or Overseas University (OU) undergraduate programmes will have the opportunity to attend an Overseas Immersion Programme (OIP) at the home campus of our OU partners. 

Students pursuing both SIT and joint degree programmes will have the opportunity to participate in the Overseas Integrated Work Study Programme (OIWSP) and other optional programmes, such as the Overseas Exposure Programme (OEP).

Find out more about the [Global Experience](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/life-at-sit/global-experience) that SIT offers.

user_input: How is WSDeg/TITO delivered at SIT (what are the durations of term-in and term-out cycles) and what student benefits are provided?
reference:
Delivery/durations:
- Delivered in a Term‑In Term‑Out (TITO) model: students alternate between university and workplace, spending between 1 to 12 months at the university and 1 to 12 months at the workplace alternately during their undergraduate studies.

Student benefits:
- Stronger grounding of theory in real work environments; more job‑ready on graduation.
- Company support may include tuition fee sponsorship, stipends, and sign‑on bonuses.
- Opportunity to learn company work culture and potential job offers upon graduation.
- Students stand to gain at least 10–16 months of accumulated relevant work experience during their degree.

They may be offered career opportunities with the partner company upon graduation. Acceptance into the SkillsFuture WSDeg (Term-In/Term-Out model) is an effective career jump-start for students as they stand to gain at least 10-16 months of accumulated relevant work experience during the course of their undergraduate studies. Row 76: S/N: 106 | Q']
response:
The WSDeg/TITO programme at SIT is delivered through a Term-In Term-Out (TITO) model, where students alternate between university and workplace, spending between one to twelve months in each term-out cycle at the workplace.  

Students benefit from a stronger grounding of theory in real work environments, tuition fee sponsorship, stipends, sign-on bonuses, exposure to company work culture, potential career offers upon graduation, and at least 10–16 months of accumulated relevant work experience during their studies.

Question: What is the minimum Cut-Off Point for SIT?
Answer: SIT practises an aptitude-based admissions selection process where applicants are not only assessed based on their academic results; we will also review their co-curricular interests, work/internship experiences, portfolios/ essays (if applicable) and interview performances, just to name a few. As such, there is no minimum cut-off point and we strongly encourage all who meet our admission requirements, and who are interested in respective programmes to apply.

Even so, selection for admissions is a competitive process for a limited number of places, and it is based on relative merit (both academically and non-academically). To keep prospective students informed of SIT's respective programmes and their chances of gaining acceptance into SIT, we have published the SIT [Indicative Grade Profile](https://singaporetech.edu.sg/files/igp) as a guide.
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025):

Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): SkillsFuture Work-Study Degree (WSDeg) or Term-In, Term-Out (TITO) for SIT students is delivered in a 'Term-In Term-Out' (TITO) model. Students will spend between one to twelve months in the university and at the workplace alternately during their undergraduate studies.

Question: How different are SIT’s programmes compared to other autonomous universities?
Answer: All SIT programmes offer undergraduates the opportunity to integrate theory and practice in their learning. Students are also able given ample opportunity to develop deep specialist skills in their chosen field through our Integrated [Work Study Programme (IWSP)](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/integrated-work-study-programme), where they undertake 8 – 12 months of relevant work experience during their course of study.

The [Overseas Integrated Work Study Programme (OIWSP)](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/life-at-sit/global-experience) is the overseas equivalent of IWSP.
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): SIT is an autonomous university in Singapore that offers industry-focused degree programmes. SIT's programmes are designed in consultation with industry leaders and feature industry-relevant curricula. SIT also has a strong emphasis on applied learning, with students gaining real-world experience through work attachments and projects with industry partners. This prepares SIT graduates to be work-ready and has resulted in strong employment outcomes, with more than 9 in 10 graduates employed within six months of graduation.

user_input: If a student's MOE TG/SIT subsidies are exhausted, will they be charged non‑subsidised fees? What steps should they take: consult the PD, use the change‑programme window, or check TG‑credit eligibility?
reference:
Yes — if you have fully utilised all MOE Tuition Grant and SIT subsidies, you will be charged non‑subsidised tuition fees (Row 79).

Recommended steps:
- Consult your current Programme Director for advice before requesting a change of programme (Row 80).  
- Apply only during the change‑of‑programme application window (mid‑January to mid‑March); applications outside the window are not accepted (Rows 80–81).  
- Check your TG‑credit eligibility by calculating remaining semesters/credits (e.g., 4/8 semesters = 50% TG eligibility) to see if any Tuition Grant still applies (context example).

Question: I am an undergraduate student from another Autonomous University, will I still be eligible for a Tuition Grant if I transfer to another full-time undergraduate degree programme in SIT? 
Answer: If are you transferring from another Autonomous University (ie. NTU, NUS, SMU, SUSS, SUTD) to a full-time SIT undergraduate degree programme and have an existing Tuition Grant from your current University, you will still be eligible for the Tuition Grant. 

Do note that it will be computed based on a pro-rated method to determine the remaining semesters or credits which qualify for the Tuition Grant. 

For example, if you have taken 4 out of 8 semesters of Tuition Grant from another Autonomous University, you will be eligible for a Tuition Grant which is 50% (4/8 semesters) of the total credits to complete the new full-time undergraduate degree programme in SIT. 
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025):

Question: What are SIT’s tuition fees like?

Please refer to [https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/tuition-fees](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/tuition-fees) for information on Tuition Fees.

Question: What is the minimum Cut-Off Point for SIT?
Answer: SIT practises an aptitude-based admissions selection process where applicants are not only assessed based on their academic results; we will also review their co-curricular interests, work/internship experiences, portfolios/ essays (if applicable) and interview performances, just to name a few. As such, there is no minimum cut-off point and we strongly encourage all who meet our admission requirements, and who are interested in respective programmes to apply.

Even so, selection for admissions is a competitive process for a limited number of places, and it is based on relative merit (both academically and non-academically). To keep prospective students informed of SIT's respective programmes and their chances of gaining acceptance into SIT, we have published the SIT [Indicative Grade Profile](https://singaporetech.edu.sg/files/igp) as a guide.
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025):

reference:
- Forecast results: Forecast results are not accepted; you must apply with your actual IB Diploma results in the next admissions exercise. (Row 52)  
- Required IB grades: Minimum grade 5 in at least two HL subjects and one SL subject, plus the IB Diploma. (Revised Answer, Row 52)  
- MTL requirements and remedies: You must satisfy the Mother Tongue Language (MTL) requirement. If unmet, you may still be admitted but must either (i) attain the stipulated MTL requirements as a private candidate, or (ii) attend equivalent courses from pre‑approved language schools before graduation. (Row 53)

Question: What are the admissions’ criteria?
Answer: SIT adopts an aptitude-based approach in assessing applicants for admission. Applicants are evaluated on their academic qualifications, co-curricular activity records and work experience. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an admission interview.

Applicants presenting the following qualifications can apply:
- A Diploma from any local Polytechnics in Singapore. 
- A Diploma from other institutions such as NAFA, LASALLE, BCA Academy and ITE will be considered on a case-by-case basis for selected programmes.
- The Singapore-Cambridge A-Levels with passes in at least 2 H2 subjects and offered General Paper or Knowledge & Inquiry (KI) in the same sitting (for selected courses).
- The International Baccalaureate with a grade five for at least 2 Higher Level (HL) and 1 Standard Level (SL) subject and had obtained the IB Diploma (for selected courses).
- International Qualifications with at least 12 years of formal education deemed as acceptable, equivalent qualifications for selected courses.

Certain programmes may also have programme-specific admission requirements. Applicants should refer to SingaporeTech.edu.sg for more information and ensure that all programme-specific requirements are met in order to be considered for admission.

reference: You may only submit an appeal for one programme; if that single-programme online appeal is unsuccessful, the result is final and you cannot re-appeal. You may apply again and be assessed afresh in the next admissions exercise. You are encouraged to improve your relative standing by acquiring relevant work experience and pursuing other endeavours that help you better understand the profession and industry associated with the degree programme.

Question: Will there be exemptions for related qualifications holder?
Answers: Guess what? We're all about recognising your brilliance, and that's why we're thrilled to share that you might catch a break with module exemptions! It's like a backstage pass to skip first-year modules – but here's the personalised touch: it's all done on a module-by-module basis.

How does it work? Well, we'll take a close look at your diploma and grades in related modules because we believe in giving credit where credit is due. So, if you've aced something similar before, we want to know about it!

But hold on, there's more good news. If you've got your hands on some shiny professional or industrial certifications, we're open to considering exemptions for those too! Because let's face it, expertise deserves recognition.

Excited about the possibility of a smoother ride through the academic journey? We are too! Let's make your path uniquely yours. 
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): Yes, you may be eligible for module exemptions based on your diploma and academic performance in relevant modules. Exemptions are assessed on a module-by-module basis.

Professional or industry certifications may also be considered for exemptions where applicable. This process ensures that prior learning and experience are appropriately recognised."]

Question: How does a student stand to benefit if he/she is accepted into WSDeg or TITO?
Answer: Students will be given a stronger grounding of theory in real work environments, equipping them to become job-ready employees right upon graduation.
While applying what they have learnt in school at work, they will also be supported by their companies. This may include, but is not restricted to, tuition fee sponsorship, stipends, and sign-on bonuses.
Students will gain the opportunity to learn more about the company’s work culture.
They may be offered career opportunities with the partner company upon graduation.
Acceptance into the SkillsFuture WSDeg (Term-In/Term-Out model) is an effective career jump-start for students as they stand to gain at least 10-16 months of accumulated relevant work experience during the course of their undergraduate studies.

Question: Do you have a Student Government/Union in SIT?
Answer: In SIT, Student Management Committees represent the respective programme cohorts and act as the voice of the students and a bridge between the university and the student body. These committees also regularly organise activities that promote bonding amongst students within and beyond the academic programmes that they represent.

SMCs organise various engagement sessions throughout the year for students within and beyond their degree programmes. Some of these engagement sessions include Annual General Meetings, talks by industry partners, and community events such as welfare pack distribution, etc.  ​

​SMCs also co-organise Day Zero (an academic induction) with their faculty and Office of SITizen Experience to welcome and induct freshmen into SIT. ​

​Some other interesting events planned by SMCs include outreach events to polytechnic students and cluster days to showcase their degree programme e.g. Engineering Day. 
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025):

Question: What is the application process and what are the important dates to note?
Answer: 1. Apply via the SIT online application portal at SingaporeTech.edu.sg

3. Check final application outcome via email notification from SIT or via the SIT online application portal

5. Successful applicants who have accepted their offer will receive a pre-matriculation package via email, and are required to complete the stipulated procedures by the deadline

Please visit our [Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate#important-dates) page to view important dates and deadlines.

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

This shift embodies our commitment to sustainable and accessible commuting options for everyone at our vibrant new location!
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.
Link provided: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Our%20%27Campus%2Din,across%20the%20campus.

Question: Where is the SIT campus located?
Answer: SIT has six campuses island-wide. The university adopts a distributed campus model where an SIT building is situated within the grounds of each of the five polytechnics. The main campus is situated at SIT@Dover which houses our administrative offices and some of SIT’s own degree programmes. We are slated to move to our brand-new Punggol campus by late 2024.
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): SIT Punggol Campus is located at 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608.

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

This shift embodies our commitment to sustainable and accessible commuting options for everyone at our vibrant new location!
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.
Link provided: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Our%20%27Campus%2Din,across%20the%20campus.
Remarks: answer could be retrieved from FAQ section under future campus page after re-crawling"]

- A comprehensive three-year degree crafted in collaboration with industry leaders, accreditation bodies, and external faculty experts; designed to refine academic knowledge, critical and analytical skills, and prepare graduates for accounting careers in commerce, industry, government, or public practice.

- Chartered Accountant/Certified Practising Accountant, Management and Taxation Consultant, Auditor, Financial and Risk Analyst, Financial Controller, Fraud Investigator, Forensic Accountant, or entrepreneur.

- Yes — applicants holding a diploma from any of the five local polytechnics are eligible to apply.

- Yes — A‑level qualifications are accepted for application.

5) Can International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma holders apply?
- Yes — IB Diploma qualifications are accepted for application.

Question: What is Bachelor of Science with Honours in Applied Computing with Honours about?
Answers: Hey Future Fintech Wizards! Ready to dive into the exciting world where technology meets finance? Our Bachelor of Science with Honours in Applied Computing, specialising in Fintech, is not just a programme – it's a journey crafted for trailblazers like you!

We've teamed up with theInfocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) and the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to make this a tech-infused financial adventure. Picture this: three years direct honours,  hands-on, paving the way for ICT talents who are about to make waves in the fintech cosmos.

What's in store for you? Well, get ready to soak up computing and finance knowledge, and emerge with a toolbox filled with practical skills that fintech dreams are made of. Software engineering, applied computing, and a sprinkle of machine learning are just the beginning. Oh, did we mention the insider scoop on banking compliance, financial products, and investment management?

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

This shift embodies our commitment to sustainable and accessible commuting options for everyone at our vibrant new location!
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.

Question: What kind of leadership opportunities are available in SIT?
Answer: The Office of SITizen Experience is always keen to work with students who are interested to take up leadership opportunities. Based on the provided knowledge base, here is the verification for each item:

- **Punggol walkways/link-bridges**: Yes. The Punggol Campus is designed for exploration on foot, featuring designated walkways and link bridges that facilitate seamless access between the two plots: SIT Campus Court and SIT Campus Heart.

- **Campus Boulevard public to L2**: Yes. Floors from the Campus Boulevard (Level 2) and below will be accessible to members of the public.

- **CIA at TP**: Yes. The Culinary Institute of America (CIA) will continue to operate from its current location at Temasek Polytechnic.

- **Phased return of SIT at Dover and at the Polytechnic**: Yes. SIT's existing campuses will gradually be returned to the respective authorities when they're no longer needed. The new centralised campus in Punggol will host all programmes except those offered by CIA.

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

This shift embodies our commitment to sustainable and accessible commuting options for everyone at our vibrant new location!
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.
Link provided: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Our%20%27Campus%2Din,across%20the%20campus.

Question: Where is the SIT campus located?
Answer: SIT has six campuses island-wide. The university adopts a distributed campus model where an SIT building is situated within the grounds of each of the five polytechnics. The main campus is situated at SIT@Dover which houses our administrative offices and some of SIT’s own degree programmes. We are slated to move to our brand-new Punggol campus by late 2024.
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): SIT Punggol Campus is located at 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608.

Questions: How does stacking to a degree work?
Answers: Our BSc (Hons) in Applied Computing degree (via the CSM Pathway) offers a flexible and modular structure comprising two foundational micro-credentials in Applied Computing, four specialised micro-credentials in key areas of the field, and two elective micro-credentials (or two specialised micro-credentials in its place).  Relevant micro-credentials that you obtain can be stacked progressively to complete this degree.

In addition to SIT’s micro-credentials, your pathway to a degree will also take into account other prior learning such as industry certifications or competencies acquired from work, which will provide you with credit or exemptions.

Visit here for more information on the degree programme.
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): CSM degree programmes equip you with a strong technical foundation and real-world experience to meet industry standards. Each micro-credential earns you a specialist certificate which you can stack towards a full degree. They are broken down into micro-credentials, allowing you to master skills that matter most to you, when you want to.

Question: Do you have a Student Government/Union in SIT?
Answer: In SIT, Student Management Committees represent the respective programme cohorts and act as the voice of the students and a bridge between the university and the student body. These committees also regularly organise activities that promote bonding amongst students within and beyond the academic programmes that they represent.

SMCs organise various engagement sessions throughout the year for students within and beyond their degree programmes. Some of these engagement sessions include Annual General Meetings, talks by industry partners, and community events such as welfare pack distribution, etc.  ​

​SMCs also co-organise Day Zero (an academic induction) with their faculty and Office of SITizen Experience to welcome and induct freshmen into SIT. ​

​Some other interesting events planned by SMCs include outreach events to polytechnic students and cluster days to showcase their degree programme e.g. Engineering Day. 
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025):

Answer: SIT adopts an aptitude-based approach in assessing applicants for admission. Applicants are evaluated on their academic qualifications, co-curricular activity records and work experience. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an admission interview.

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

This shift embodies our commitment to sustainable and accessible commuting options for everyone at our vibrant new location!
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.

Questions: What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
Answers: As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Here's what you can expect in terms of transportation options:

MRT: The Punggol Coast MRT Station, situated right in front of SIT's Library Building, is slated to be up and running by the end of 2024.

Public buses: The Punggol Coast Bus Interchange is anticipated to be ready by the end of 2024. In the meantime, discussions with LTA are underway to provide interim feeder bus services from Punggol MRT station until the Punggol Coast MRT station is ready.

Cars: Limited parking will be available in a shared underground carpark with JTC Business Park, along with limited season parking.

Bicycles & Personal Mobility Devices: There will be dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, strategically located across the campus.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.

Question: What kind of counselling support is available to SIT students?
Answer: Counselling services are available to all matriculated SIT students free-of-charge. Students are welcome to schedule a session to speak to a professional counsellor about the challenges they face, develop strategies, discover strengths, and gain a deeper understanding of themselves. Counselling sessions take place either in-person on campus (SIT@Dover) or virtually (via Zoom). 

The SIT Counselling Service is located at the SIT Student Life Office, SIT@Dover, University Services Centre, Level 1. 

Operating Hours are:
Monday to Thursday - 8:30AM to 6PM (Last appointment is at 5:00PM)
Friday - 8:30AM to 5:30PM (Last appointment is at 4:30PM)
SIT Counselling Service 24-hour Helpline: 6592 2030 

To make an appointment, please email SITCounselling@Singaporetech.edu.sg or walk-in to SIT Counselling Service at the Office of SITizen Experience
Revised Answer (AS OF SEP 2025): Yes, there is counselling support available to SIT students. Counselling is confidential and free for all students.
